title: Bot-detection retry and 429 backoff for static fetcher
description: Add retry logic to SimpleFetcher for bot blocks and rate limits. When a 403 with cf-mitigated:challenge or cf-ray header is received, retry with a different UA (or escalate). On 429, apply exponential backoff with jitter before retrying. Currently scrpr does a single attempt and hard-fails. Reference: opencode's webfetch retries with honest UA on Cloudflare 403s.

title: Add native sx to scrpr pipeline integration
description:
No native integration between sx and scrpr. Users need to manually extract URLs.

Solutions to consider:
1. Add --pipe flag to sx that outputs URLs formatted for scrpr
2. Add --from-json flag to scrpr to read sx JSON output
3. Document recommended pipeline patterns in both READMEs

Example: sx 'query' --pipe | scrpr --batch

title: Add response size limits to prevent OOM on large pages
description: Cap response bodies at 5MB (configurable) in the static fetcher. Currently SimpleFetcher does io.ReadAll(resp.Body) with no limit, so a malicious or broken page can exhaust memory. Use io.LimitReader or check Content-Length before reading.

title: Replace hand-rolled Markdown converter with battle-tested library
description: The convertToMarkdown() function in processor.go is ~200 lines of recursive goquery tree-walking. It is brittle for nested lists, tables, inline code, and nested formatting. Replace it with a library like github.com/JohannesKaufmann/html-to-markdown which handles edge cases correctly. Reference: opencode uses turndown (mature JS library).

title: Document extraction backend selection guide
description: Document extraction backend selection guide. Explain when to use readability (default, fast, local), Tavily (better quality, handles JS, API credits), Jina (free, no auth, rate limits). Include comparison table, cost considerations, and example use cases for each backend.

title: Complete file output implementation
description: File output exists as TODO comment instead of implementation. Need to implement -o for single file and -o dir for multiple files with auto-naming. Support both text and markdown formats. Handle file naming collisions properly.

title: Add --no-follow-redirects flag
description:
No option to disable redirect following. Currently hardcoded to follow redirects.

Solution: Add --no-follow-redirects flag to control redirect behavior. Default should remain true for backward compatibility. Document in README.

title: Auto-create config file on first run
description:
# Problem
Unlike sx, scrpr does not auto-create config.toml on first run.

# Proposed Solution
- Check for config on startup
- If not exists, create default config at $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/scrpr/config.toml
- Populate with sensible defaults
- Notify user that config was created

# Related Code
cmd/scrpr/main.go - initConfig function

title: Format-aware Accept headers for content negotiation
description: Send q-weighted Accept headers based on the requested output format. For markdown: text/markdown;q=1.0, text/plain;q=0.8, text/html;q=0.7. For text: text/plain;q=1.0, text/html;q=0.8. This lets sites that support content negotiation serve the preferred format directly. Currently scrpr sends one generic Accept header.

title: Add continue-on-error flag for pipelines
description:
When processing multiple URLs via pipe, failures break the entire pipeline. One bad URL stops processing.

Solution: Add --continue-on-error flag. When enabled, log error but continue processing remaining URLs. Return non-zero exit code at end if any errors occurred. Default should be true for piped input.

title: Implement progress bar for batch operations
description: The --progress flag is documented in help but not actually implemented. Need to add progress bar for batch URL processing. Consider using schollz/progressbar or similar library. Show progress when processing multiple URLs with -f or piped input.

title: Add quiet/silent mode flag
description:
# Problem
No --quiet flag to suppress error messages for script usage.

# Proposed Solution
- Add -q/--quiet flag
- When enabled, suppress all non-content output to stderr
- Still return non-zero exit code on errors
- Document in README for scripting best practices

# Usage Example
sx 'query' -p -L | scrpr --quiet 2>/dev/null

title: Complete config file implementation
description:
Config file has rate_limit and parallel sections but they're not fully implemented.

Need to implement:
- rate_limit.delay between requests
- parallel.max_concurrency 
- parallel.show_progress flag
- parallel.fail_fast option

Ensure CLI flags can override config settings.

title: Implement granular exit codes
description:
# Problem
All failures exit with code 1. Can't distinguish between different failure types in scripts.

# Proposed Solution
Define exit codes:
- 0: Success
- 1: Network error (connection failed, DNS, timeout)
- 2: Parse/processing error (content extraction failed)
- 3: Invalid URL or input
- 4: Configuration error
- 5: File I/O error

# Benefits
- Scripts can handle different errors differently
- Better debugging information
- Follows Unix conventions

title: Implement rate limiting between requests
description:
No rate limiting between requests. When piping multiple URLs, could hammer target sites.

Solution: Implement the existing rate_limit.delay config option from config.toml. Add --delay flag to CLI. Default to 0.5s between requests. Show warning if processing many URLs without delay.

title: Add Jina Reader API backend
description: Add Jina Reader API (r.jina.ai) as an extraction backend. Jina Reader converts any URL to clean Markdown without requiring an API key. Simple GET request to https://r.jina.ai/{URL}. Rate limited but free. Good fallback when Tavily credits run out or for simple extraction tasks.

title: Short-circuit image URLs instead of piping through readability
description: If a user passes an image URL (e.g. .png, .jpg), scrpr currently pipes the binary bytes through readability and produces garbage. Detect image/* content-type responses early and either save the image directly or return a useful message. Reference: opencode detects image responses and returns them as base64 file attachments.

title: Add Tavily Extract API backend
description: Add Tavily Extract API as an extraction backend option alongside local readability extraction. Tavily provides cloud-based content extraction that handles JavaScript-heavy sites better than local go-readability. Endpoint: POST https://api.tavily.com/extract with Bearer auth. Cost: 1 credit per 5 URLs (basic) or 2 credits per 5 URLs (advanced).

title: Auto-escalate to Jina backend when local extraction fails
description: Add a fallback chain for extraction: try local readability first, and on bot-block or empty-content failure automatically try the free Jina Reader backend before giving up. This keeps the default path fast while improving success rate on JS-heavy or protected sites without requiring the user to pass -B explicitly.
